title: festuca valesiaca subsp. parviflora
intro: Festuca valesiaca subsp. parviflora, also known as alpine fescue or small-flowered fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Festuca valesiaca subsp. parviflora include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
